hypothesis: If EfficientNet-B0 is first pretrained on a data-rich set of additive-free NCM SEM images and then fine-tuned on the small additive-induced SEM dataset with class balancing, then it will achieve higher accuracy and F1 in classifying the nine composition-aging classes than models trained only on the target data or without stage-1 pretraining, because transferable additive-invariant morphological features (e.g., particle cracking and surface boundaries) learned from the source domain improve generalization in the data-poor domain.
